lens: Iteration 5 — COLD READ of /home/renaud/Ledger/Ledger_Spec_v13.0/ledger/ledger_v13_0.tex as a first-time reader: no R1–R4 residue visible; §3 domain rulings respected; no broken refs/listings
material: Stale hard-coded section number '(sec.8)' for the futures lifecycle: ledger_v13_0.tex:2226 ('the three-home model (sec.4) and the futures lifecycle (sec.8)') and :2313 ('Product guards ... live in handle (sec.8, G1-G5)'). All \ref{sec:futures} citations resolve correctly to 9 — only these two hand-numbered code comments point to 8, a misdirected reference visible to a cold reader and a scar of an inserted section shifting the numbering (R3 / broken ref).

Cross-reference to absent material plus undeclared property number: ledger_v13_0.tex:3130 cites 'the retro-insertion permutation oracle (P24 family, Appendix~B/app:properties)' and :2551 cites the same oracle in that appendix. Appendix app:properties contains no retro-insertion permutation oracle (the only occurrences of 'retro-insertion'/'permutation' in the document are the two citing sites), and the canonical numbering is fixed as P1–P23 (:5866, :5965; glossary :6717 'Property identifiers P1–P23'). 'P24' exists nowhere else; the appendix pointer dangles (R3: cross-reference assuming absent material / broken ref).
notes: Full 7,786-line cold read; section numbers verified against ledger_v13_0.aux (lifecycle=7, basis=8, futures=9); build log shows zero undefined references/citations. Clean on R1 (title page = title/subtitle/author/date only; no version identity in prose, comments, or PDF metadata — the v13_0 file/directory names sit outside the document text and outside read-only scope), R2/R4 (no change-driven or compatibility prose; all 'no longer'/'superseded'/'legacy' occurrences describe modelled objects or real bank systems during deployment migration, per the §3 do-not-touch list), and R5 (all 5 bibliography entries cited; no TODO/FIXME; all LaTeX comments are benign figure/typography annotations). Domain vocabulary fully intact: TermsVersion/appendVersion/currentTerms (versioned ProductTerms by design), usSupersededBy/SetSupersededBy/DanglingSupersede/supersedeTx (C8 lifecycle facts), C8 breaking amendments. Correct hard-coded refs verified: 'Sec. 2'/'Sec. 4' comments, 'Property~5' (:1864) and 'Property~6' (:4981) match the §1 enumeration, 'FAQ Q1' (:4927) matches. The repeated Qty/Move/Transaction listings across sections are explicitly framed as one woven reference (reference/Ledger.hs) and read as deliberate Hutton-style presentation, not merged-draft duplication. Minor non-material blemishes for the next edit iteration: (a) :1132 'Section~\ref{sec:lifecycle}, \S7' renders as the stutter 'Section 7, §7' — consistent target, likely a renumbering leftover; (b) :2835 code comment '(Invariant B: ...)' uses a name defined nowhere (the invariant is Single-basis consumption / C13); content is restated inline so the reader is not misdirected, but the nickname resolves to nothing and risks collision with 'design B' of §4. File not edited (read-only mandate observed). Remediation for the two material findings is mechanical: replace 'sec.8' with 'sec.9' (or non-numeric 'the futures lifecycle section') at :2226/:2313, and either add the retro-insertion permutation oracle to app:properties under a declared number or drop the 'P24 family' designation and appendix pointer at :2551/:3130.

lens: Cold read as a first-time reader: single voice, present-tense statements of what IS; no sentence presupposes a prior state of the text (R1–R5 residue classes, domain vocabulary protected).
notes:
Full sequential read of /home/renaud/Ledger/Ledger_Spec_v13.0/ledger/ledger_v13_0.tex (7,786 lines) plus pattern sweeps and mechanical integrity checks. No edits made.

VERIFIED CLEAN — R1: title page is title/author/date only; no version, revision, or dated-revision language anywhere in prose, headers, comments, or PDF metadata (pdfinfo Title/Subject empty; the only version identity is the filename, an owner item outside the .tex). R2: every "now", "old", "no longer", "thereafter", "replaced", "updated" describes a MODELLED OBJECT (ex-coupon bonds, collateral eligibility, positions going flat, custody matching, a source publishing at the old basis point, CDM version coexistence) — none describes the text's own evolution. R3: no remark justifies a change or reconciles with a superseded structure; the intra-document layering ("UnitStatus gains one field", "the closed status-writer set, extended by ONE case", "SBL adds ten invariants") is forward deductive development within one version, licensed by the declared scalar-to-vector progression, not merged-draft scar tissue. R4: no backward-compatibility, deprecation, or transition-period language for the document; "legacy systems" (2 sites) means real bank systems in a deployment migration — protected. R5: all comments are typesetting/diagram rationale; no TODO/FIXME, no draft/agent references, no commented-out blocks; bibliography exactly matches citations (no orphans either way); no duplicate labels or hypertargets; every \ref/\cite/\hyperref resolves; fresh build log (12:35, post-dating the .tex) is free of errors, warnings, and undefined references. Protected domain vocabulary (TermsVersion/appendVersion/currentTerms, supersedeTx/SetSupersededBy/DanglingSupersede/C8, satellite-specification scoping, CDM v6.0.0 pinning, IBP identifiers) intact throughout.

NON-MATERIAL observations (register only, no prior-state implication; recorded in agent memory, left untouched under constraint 8): four second-person plain-reading glosses (lines ~2329, ~5356, ~5773 prose; ~3249 code comment) and one first-person code comment ("we SUM", ~3471); TikZ wallet label "Our Portfolio" (~669); bullet head "Protected elements untouched" (~3146) parses as a conservativity claim but faintly echoes review vocabulary. None is residue visible as document history, none alters meaning, damages vocabulary, or breaks a reference — hence not material under this lens; they are candidates only if a separate register pass is ever commissioned.

lens: NOETHER — cold-read structural integrity: every theorem/invariant/proof/definition survives with meaning intact; no simplification weakened a statement; no residue, broken reference, or damaged listing visible to a first-time reader.
material: Stale hardcoded section pointers 'sec.8' in Section 7 listing comments (tex lines 2226 and 2313, /home/renaud/Ledger/Ledger_Spec_v13.0/ledger/ledger_v13_0.tex): '-- ...the futures lifecycle (sec.8)' and '-- handle (sec.8, G1-G5)'. The Futures Lifecycle — where futHandle and the G1–G5 guards live (lines 3156, 3274, 3798–3825) — is Section 9; Section 8 is The State-Basis Discipline. Both comments send a cold reader to the wrong section; the parallel comments 'sec.4' (three-home model) and 'Section 2' (ledger) are correct, so this is a numbering scar from a prior structure in which futures preceded the state-basis section. Fix: sec.8 → sec.9 in both comments.

Phantom proof witness 'retro-insertion permutation oracle' / 'P24 family' (tex lines 2550–2551 and 3129–3130). The tip-weld agreement claim — booking-order status fold equals effective-order chain projection on usBasis at every log prefix — is stated as 'witnessed by the retro-insertion permutation oracle (Appendix~\ref{app:properties}), not merely asserted' and again as '(P24 family, Appendix~\ref{app:properties})'. Appendix B (Property Test Catalogue, lines 6407–6582) contains only the P1–P10 oracles plus two unnumbered totality oracles and names extensions P11–P23; no P24 exists anywhere in the document (the canonical numbering is fixed as P1–P23 at line 5866), and no retro-insertion or permutation oracle appears in the appendix. As written, the 'not merely asserted' claim is unsupported — an invariant-support statement weakened by a dangling reference. Either the oracle must exist in Appendix B or the two sentences must drop the citation and the 'P24 family' name.

Duplicated section pointer 'Section 7, §7' (tex line 1132): 'the immutable event log is (justified by the idempotent replay of Section~\ref{sec:lifecycle}, \S7)' renders in the PDF as 'Section 7, §7' (pdftotext line 1699) — a stale hardcoded '§7' left standing beside the live \ref to the same section. Merged-draft residue visible to a cold reader; delete ', \S7'.

Raw LaTeX markup rendered verbatim inside a listing (tex line 4660, Settlement Layer): the comment '-- The boundary type. NOT the core Transaction (the log event of Sec.~ledger):' sits inside a lstlisting, so the PDF prints the literal string 'Sec.~ledger' — tilde included (pdftotext line 6147). A reader sees unprocessed markup and an unresolvable section name; should read 'Sec. 2' or 'the ledger section' in plain text.
notes: Verified clean: all \ref/\autoref/\hyperref targets resolve against defined labels (comm check empty); all \hyperlink targets have \hypertargets; all 5 bibitems cited and all cites defined; build log free of undefined-reference warnings; no TODO/FIXME/commented-out blocks; title page carries only title/author/date with no version identity in content or PDF metadata; no R2/R4 change-driven or compatibility prose found (all 'versioned/superseded/no longer' hits are domain vocabulary — TermsVersion, appendVersion, usSupersededBy, ex-coupon — correctly left intact). Mathematical integrity confirmed end to end: Theorem 2.4 (System Closure) proof matches statement; P10 telescoping proof sound; Theorem 8.x basis value-invariance identity (qf)(p-c)/f = qp - qc algebraically correct with weld hypotheses stated; fee zero-sum, segregation (CONS ∧ LOC ∧ C4), TRS-equivalence sketch, obligation-liveness composition L1–L5, and the futures closing identity (A=+2100, B=-1700, C=-400, sum 0, cross-checked against trade-level PnL) all verified; worked-example arithmetic (managed account 5,750/28,850/1,115,400; futures VM table; eight avail scenarios; EU/US SBL conservation sums) recomputed and correct; P1–P23 and C1–C13 numbering consistent across hub (Sec. 17), local sections, and glossary; 'Property 5/6' references correctly resolve to the six-property list of Sec. 1; FAQ Q1/Q2 back-references resolve. Borderline items not counted material: (a) listing comment '(Invariant B: beta_t is total...)' at tex 2835 references an invariant name defined nowhere (the invariant is 'Single-basis consumption', inv:basis) — the parenthetical restates the content inline so the reader loses nothing, but the stale name is the same brittle-tag class as the sec.8 failure; (b) hardcoded 'Sec. 4'/'[Section 4]' in Unit Store listing comments (tex 836, 849) are currently correct but share the brittleness that produced finding 1 — converting all in-listing section pointers to stable prose names would close the class; (c) the artifact filename ledger_v13_0.tex/pdf carries a version designation (R1), owned by the packaging lens rather than this one. File read-only; no edits made.

lens: MATTHIAS — cold read, Iteration 5: code/prose coherence; no orphaned identifier; listings uniform and consistent with the text
material: M1 — Broken hardcoded section pointers in §7 (Lifecycle Management) listing comments, lines 2226 and 2313: 'the futures lifecycle (sec.8)' and 'Product guards ... live in handle (sec.8, G1-G5)'. The futures lifecycle — the only place G1–G5 exist (futHandle, lines 3799–3825) — is Section 9 in the compiled document; Section 8 is The State-Basis Discipline, which contains no G1–G5. A first-time reader following either pointer lands in the wrong section. (All other hardcoded numbers — 'Sec. 2', 'Sec. 4', '§7' — are correct; this pair looks like a numbering shift left behind when the basis section was interposed.)

M2 — Orphaned identifier 'P24' and a cross-reference to absent material. Line 3130 cites 'the retro-insertion permutation oracle (P24 family, Appendix app:properties)' and line 2550–2551 cites the same oracle in the same appendix. The invariant hub twice fixes the canonical numbering as P1–P23 (lines 5866, 5965; glossary line 6717 likewise says P1–P23); no P24 is defined anywhere, and Appendix app:properties contains neither a P24 entry nor any retro-insertion/permutation oracle — grep confirms 'retro-insertion' occurs only at the two citing sites. Both the identifier and the promised oracle dangle.

M3 — The two listings of the 'closed' StatusWrite type disagree beyond the announced extension. §4 (lines 1244–1253) defines StatusWrite with 'three constructors each name one field', SetLastSettle Qty, applyStatus writing usLastSettle :: Maybe Qty — and the §4 remark claims these listings are 'excerpted verbatim from reference/Ledger.hs', which indeed carries SetLastSettle Qty (reference/Ledger.hs lines 295–313). §8 (lines 2511–2518) re-lists the type as 'the closed status-writer set, extended by ONE case', and the prose (Definition of usBasis, line 2501–2505) says UnitStatus is 'written only through the closed StatusWrite set by one new constructor' — but the listing makes TWO changes: it adds SetBasis AND silently retypes SetLastSettle to (Price, BasisPoint), justified only by an inline comment. No updated UnitStatus record (usLastSettle's new field type) and no updated applyStatus equation are ever shown, so §4's applyStatus cannot apply §8's constructor and the document's two versions of a type it calls closed cannot both hold. A cold reader reconstructing the system hits a type-level contradiction between two sections and the reference file.
notes: Scope read: entire file (7,786 lines) plus reference/Ledger.hs, the compiled .toc, and the LaTeX log (no undefined references, no multiply-defined labels, no overfull boxes). No R1–R5 residue was observed through this lens: no version identity, no change-driven prose, no TODO/FIXME or commented-out blocks; the versioned-by-design vocabulary (TermsVersion, appendVersion, usSupersededBy, supersedeTx, DanglingSupersede, C8, 'legacy systems' as real bank systems, modelled-object 'no longer') is used correctly throughout and was not misread as residue. Identifier hygiene is otherwise excellent: every Haskell name introduced in a listing is either defined in-document, attributed to reference/Ledger.hs, or explicitly declared a boundary type (Fut*, SettlementTx, CdmTransaction, OracleTx); the negQty/qneg aliasing is declared once in §2 and honored (§4/§10/§11 use qneg, §2/§13 use negQty). Non-material notes for the owner: (a) line 1132 reads 'Section~\ref{sec:lifecycle}, §7' which compiles to the redundant 'Section 7, §7'; (b) the withSnapshot listing comment (line 2835) says 'Invariant B' where the text elsewhere says 'Invariant (Single-basis consumption)' — an informal alias a cold reader must guess; (c) glossary preamble says 'condition identifiers C1–C12' while the same page acknowledges C13 — the sentence undercounts by one; (d) OpSpec comment 'Scale Rational -- q /= 0' (line 2584) names the scale factor q where the illustrations call it f; (e) EU SBL worked example Step 3 (lines 7186, 7200): the prose 'Goldman, owning the Bunds under title transfer, re-uses...' sits against the appendix's own regime table ('title transfer: receiver owns collateral') and Step 1's narration ('the poster transfers ownership'); the moves, the double-short narration, and the conservation sums are internally consistent and clearly deliberate, so under constraint 8 (design/content not under review) this is flagged as a content tension for the domain owners, not counted as a material coherence failure of this certification. pass=false on M1–M3; all three are small, surgical fixes (two comment pointers 'sec.8'→'sec.9'; either define the P24 family/oracle in Appendix app:properties or rephrase the two citations to name an obligation, not an existing artefact; state the SetLastSettle retype explicitly in §8 prose and show the updated UnitStatus/applyStatus, or keep SetLastSettle Qty and carry the basis stamp elsewhere).

notes: Everything else certifies clean under this lens. REFERENCES: 238 labels, zero duplicates; every \ref (113 unique targets) and every \hyperref target resolves (comm of extracted labels vs refs empty; log has no undefined/multiply-defined warnings); \cite set exactly equals \bibitem set (5/5); all nine appendices are referenced from the body — no orphaned appendix; spot-checked semantic correctness of prose ordinals: \"Property 5\" (Lifecycle Value Invariance, line 1864) and \"Property 6\" (Time Travel, line 4981) match the \S1 enumeration; Theorem/Invariant refs (inv:P1, inv:P10, inv:P20--P23, cond:C1--C13, thm:*, prin:*) point to the objects they name. STRUCTURE: front matter (Preface, Abstract, ToC, Roadmap, Key Concepts) then Parts I--VI then Appendices then References; the announced scalar-to-vector progression is honoured (vector waits for Part V); each major part closes with \"Key Invariants and Consequences\"; P1--P23/C1--C13 numbering is consistent with one canonical hub (\S17); ordering reads as designed, not accreted. RESIDUE SWEEP: R1 clean (title/author/date only; \"July 2026\"; PDF metadata carries no version); R2/R4 clean — every \"no longer\"/\"now\"/supersede/legacy instance describes a modelled object (ex-coupon bonds, collateral eligibility, unit succession) or real bank systems during deployment migration, all in the DO-NOT-TOUCH class; R3: no duplicate statements in different registers found beyond the pointer scars above; R5 clean — comments are technical build/navigation only, no TODO/FIXME, no commented-out blocks, no unused packages (booktabs 227 rule uses, longtable 35, tikz 10, enumitem 49), bibliography contains nothing uncited. Peripheral observation outside the read-only document text: the filename ledger_v13_0.tex and directory Ledger_Spec_v13.0 carry version identity (R1-adjacent) — flagged for the owner since a renaming is outside this certification's mandate. All three material findings are one-line comment fixes (sec.8 -> sec.9 twice; drop \", \\S7\"; \"Sec.~ledger\" -> \"sec:ledger\"); no design content is implicated.
